prerequisites: An Airbyte instance (Cloud or self-managed) where you can add and configure a destination.
An Airbyte source and connection to send records to this destination, since its only purpose is testing source/sync behavior.
A choice of test mode for the required test_destination field (the connector supports Silent /dev/null, Logging, Throttling, and Failing modes).

authMethods.name: No authentication
authMethods.summary: The dev/null testing destination requires no credentials. The connector descriptor declares zero secret fields, and the Airbyte documentation lists no API keys, tokens, or login. Records are discarded, logged, throttled, or used to trigger a deliberate failure entirely within the connector, so there is no external system to authenticate against.

setupSteps.title: Add the End-to-End Testing (/dev/null) destination
setupSteps.body: In Airbyte, create a new destination and select End-to-End Testing (/dev/null). This destination is intended for testing Airbyte connections: it can act as a /dev/null sink, log source records, or mimic specific behaviors such as an exception during a sync. No credentials are requested because the connector has no secret fields.

setupSteps.title: Configure the required test_destination mode
setupSteps.body: Set the required test_destination field to the behavior you want to test. The documented modes are: Silent (/dev/null), which discards all incoming records and is the default on Airbyte Cloud; Logging, which records up to 1,000 entries (first N, every N-th, or random sampling); Throttling, which adds a configurable millisecond delay between messages to mimic a slow sync; and Failing, which throws an exception after a specified number of messages. Note that the Logging mode may expose sensitive data in logs.

setupSteps.title: Create a connection and run a test sync
setupSteps.body: Attach the destination to a connection from your chosen source and trigger a sync to validate source extraction, throughput, or error-handling behavior. Because the destination does not persist data to any external store, use it only for end-to-end testing rather than real data delivery.
